title: herniaria boissieri var. frigida
intro: Herniaria boissieri var. frigida, also known as the alpine riddell, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating skin conditions and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
